title: Celtis insularis
taxonomy: Celtis insularis is classified as a species of plant in the family Cannabaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as hemp or nettle trees. However, some sources may group it under the family Ulmaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as elm trees. The genus Celtis is a group of plants within the family Cannabaceae or Ulmaceae, and it includes around 60-70 species of trees and shrubs. These plants are native to various parts of the world, including Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The classification of Celtis insularis may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered a member of the genus Celtis within the family Cannabaceae or Ulmaceae.
uses: Celtis insularis, a plant native to the Mediterranean region, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. The leaves and bark of Celtis insularis contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant's extracts are also reported to have antimicrobial and antiviral activities, which may help in wound healing and prevention of infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Celtis insularis and its efficacy in treating various health conditions.
